name: Vasco Smith
title: Vasco Smith
description: systems, rust, windows internals. the netherlands.
bio: kernel drivers, compiler passes, small interpreters, quic experiments. mostly c++ and rust. interested in what sits under the abstraction: allocators, codegen, syscalls.

projects.title: kernel-anticheat
projects.description: Windows kernel anti-cheat for driver integrity testing and security research.
projects.cvDescription: Ring-0 Windows driver built as a reference anti-cheat for driver integrity testing and security research. Scans for cheat signals via process handle stripping, module enumeration, and signature checks.

projects.title: x86-obfuscator
projects.description: Rust CLI for x86-64 PE binary obfuscation with a custom lifter.
projects.cvDescription: Rust CLI that obfuscates x86-64 PE binaries through a custom lifter, applying transformations on the IR before reassembly.

projects.title: shared-section-driver
projects.description: Windows kernel driver demonstrating kernel-to-usermode communication via shared memory sections.
projects.cvDescription: Windows kernel driver that establishes shared memory sections mapped into both ring-0 and ring-3, demonstrating low-overhead kernel-to-usermode communication.

projects.title: quic-remote-memory
projects.description: Rust proof-of-concept for streaming memory reads over a QUIC transport.
projects.cvDescription: Proof-of-concept that streams remote process memory reads over QUIC using `quinn`.

projects.title: llvm-obfuscator
projects.description: LLVM pass for code obfuscation via MBA and control-flow flattening.
projects.cvDescription: Out-of-tree LLVM passes applying mixed boolean-arithmetic transformations and control-flow flattening at the IR level. Plugs into a stock clang toolchain.

projects.title: doh-edge
projects.description: DNS resolver with edge-local caching, built on Workers + Durable Objects.
projects.cvDescription: DNS-over-HTTPS resolver running on Cloudflare Workers, with edge-local caching backed by Durable Objects for per-region state.

bullets: Worked on a geo-targeted proxy service that resells upstream pools (Oxylabs and similar); clients query proxies by country for scraping and localization workloads.

Triaged and patched the original Go collector, then rewrote it in Rust after the Go version leaked memory under long-lived load. The service refreshes the pool from upstream providers, probes liveness, and indexes geo metadata so the API can serve per-country selections.
A typical snapshot tracked ~140k proxies across ~133k unique outbound IPs, with duplicate detection and health checks driven from Redis.

cvSkills.label: Systems
cvSkills.value: Rust, Go, C++, x86-64 assembly, LLVM
